description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 threat & injection cluster: sensitive-data disclosure/redaction vs retrieval authorization vs credential custody vs tenant surface audit, and against the shipped secrets-identity-hardener and tenant-isolation-reviewer.

cases.prompt: Strip PII and secrets out of what we send to the model and make sure the model can't read sensitive input back to the user.
cases.reason: Data minimization/redaction of what enters context and leaks in output is this skill; rag-security-architect controls retrieval permission and secrets-identity-hardener owns credential custody in the codebase.

cases.prompt: Ensure a user only retrieves vector-store documents their tenant and role are authorized to access.
cases.reason: Who can retrieve which documents is rag-security-architect (LLM08); this skill redacts/minimizes sensitive data regardless of retrieval permission.

cases.prompt: We have API keys and DB credentials scattered in env vars and one leaked into the client bundle — harden secret custody and rotate.
cases.reason: Secret custody, env classification, and rotation is secrets-identity-hardener; this skill composes it but focuses on sensitive data reaching the model/output/logs.

cases.prompt: Our system prompt contains an internal API key so the model can call our API — can an attacker extract it?
cases.reason: Secrets in the system prompt and their extraction is system-prompt-leakage-reviewer (LLM07); this skill covers sensitive data in the user/context/output/log flow, not the prompt itself.

cases.prompt: Audit our whole multi-tenant system for cross-tenant leakage across all surfaces — storage, logs, search, jobs, exports.
cases.reason: A whole-system multi-surface tenant-isolation audit is tenant-isolation-reviewer; this skill composes it for the AI-disclosure surface specifically, not the full estate.
